title: How to use V{prefix}__ALL.a library
body:
I have in the past used verilator with the --exe option to directly create an executable from a top-level verilog module and a C++ file with a main function. However, now I want my C++ program to connect to two separate top-level modules at the same time. I quickly found that this cannot be done with the --exe option since verilator handles only one top-level module at a time. So I ran verilator twice -- once for each top-level module -- to create two V{prefix}__ALL.a libraries that my C++ program can link to. Unfortunately, I cannot figure out how to properly compile and link my C++ manually, and there appears to be no documentation on this. When I naively compile with `g++ main.cpp VModA__ALL.a VModB__ALL.a`, I get an error that 'verilated.h' is not found, and if I include `$VERILATOR_ROOT/include`, then I get a long string of linker errors. When I used the --exe option in the past, I noticed my C++ program was compiled with quite a lot of g++ options that I don't fully understand, so I am unsure how to compile/link my C++ program manually.

Is there a recommended way to manually compile/link C++ programs for verilator? Is there a recommended format for a custom makefile that will call the verilator makefiles and compile/link the C++ program? Am I even approaching this properly? Perhaps some more documentation is in order, since all the documentation/examples/tutorials that I could find use the --exe option.
